macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Oil prices jump on Middle East tensions, lifting inflation fears
items.summary: Crude oil prices rose sharply in early trading, stoking concerns that higher energy costs could push global inflation higher and complicate central bank policy decisions. The move comes amid heightened geopolitical risk around the Strait of Hormuz and broader Middle East conflict.

items.title: Banking sector dips as BMO and peers retreat
items.summary: The banking sector declined in recent trading, with Bank of Montreal (BMO) down about 1.6%, reflecting broader risk‑off sentiment and pressure on financials amid elevated inflation and uncertainty about future rate cuts.

items.title: Household spending growth slows, fixed investment contracts
items.summary: Household spending growth eased to 0.5% from 1.0% in the prior quarter, while fixed investment contracted by 1.9%, dragged down by sharp declines in housing investment and intellectual property rights, even as machinery and transport investment rebounded.

items.title: Trump warns of targeting Iranian energy infrastructure if Strait of Hormuz remains closed
items.summary: Former President Donald Trump warned that the U.S. could strike Iran’s electric plants, oil wells, and Kharg Island if a deal is not reached and the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened, escalating geopolitical risk and adding to energy‑market volatility.

items.title: Rupiah heads for monthly loss on strong dollar and fiscal worries
items.summary: Indonesia’s rupiah is on track for a monthly decline, pressured by a strong U.S. dollar and concerns about the country’s fiscal position, even as sentiment was partially supported by stronger‑than‑expected Chinese manufacturing data.

items.title: South Korean won drops 1.24% in FX markets
items.summary: The South Korean won fell 1.24%, among the worst‑performing currencies, as global risk aversion and dollar strength weighed on Asian FX markets.

items.title: Markets slide in March on Middle East‑driven inflation fears
items.summary: Major equity markets posted a 7.8% drop in March, their first monthly decline in four months and the weakest since January 2022, driven by inflation concerns linked to the widening Middle East conflict and higher oil prices.

items.title: RBA minutes signal further tightening if oil stays near $100
items.summary: Reserve Bank of Australia minutes showed policymakers expect additional tightening if oil prices remain around $100 per barrel, warning that inflation could reach 5% in the second quarter under such conditions.

items.title: April inflation and jobs data in focus as Fed enters new decision window
items.summary: Markets are closely watching upcoming April CPI and PPI releases, which will round out the inflation picture after the Fed held rates at 3.50–3.75% at its April 29 meeting, citing elevated inflation partly driven by higher global energy prices.

items.title: G7 trade ministers meet in Paris amid geopolitical and trade tensions
items.summary: G7 trade ministers gathered in Paris for high‑stakes talks, with rising geopolitical tensions, trade disputes, and the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz dominating the agenda and adding pressure on global markets.
items.source: YouTube – Iran Crisis & US Tariffs Add Pressure On Markets

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Altcoins Surge Amid Steady Bitcoin Rise
items.summary: The crypto market capitalization has increased by 0.75% in the past 24 hours, reaching $2.69 trillion, with altcoins like Zcash leading gains at +29%.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Above $81,000 Following ETF Inflows
items.summary: Bitcoin price remains above $81,000 after two consecutive days of heavy ETF inflows and a pause in geopolitical tensions related to the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Ethereum Maintains $2,300 Level Despite Weak Retail Demand
items.summary: Ether, the second-largest cryptocurrency, is down less than 1% in 24 hours but has held steady above $2,300 despite persistent bearish retail sentiment.

items.title: Crypto Futures Liquidations Reach $363 Million in 24 Hours
items.summary: Significant liquidations of $363 million occurred across major crypto futures exchanges in the past 24 hours, with $101 million liquidated in the most recent hour.

items.title: Bitcoin Tests $90,000 as Profit-Taking Accelerates
items.summary: Bitcoin has returned above $80,000 with profit-taking accelerating, setting up a critical test of whether institutional demand can push the asset toward $90,000.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: AI Identity Fraud Poses Major Business Risk in New Zealand
items.summary: New research from tech firm Lumin reveals that over 55% of New Zealand organizations were hit by AI-powered fraud in the past year, with average costs of $2.2 million annually. The study found that 90% of businesses fear their critical agreement workflows are vulnerable to AI-powered fraud attacks.

items.title: OpenAI's Hallucination Rates Increasing with Newer Models
items.summary: Analysis reveals OpenAI's internal benchmarks show hallucination rates rising from 16% to 48% in newer models, with the company measuring but not publicly explaining the degradation. The issue stems from the industry's reliance on synthetic data training, which may be amplifying errors rather than solving them.

items.title: Hermes Agent Outperforms OpenClaw with Enhanced Reliability
items.summary: Recent updates to Hermes Agent have made it a more reliable and powerful AI agent than OpenClaw, with new functionality including slash goal capabilities previously only available in OpenAI's CodeEx. The agent now offers improved 24/7 autonomous operation with enhanced reliability.

items.title: Global South Could Redefine AI Development Beyond Hardware Concentration
items.summary: AI researcher Randy Goebel argues that the current trajectory of massive models, energy consumption, and capital concentration is unsustainable, and that the Global South could lead AI innovation by focusing on intellectual capital rather than hardware. The discussion highlights sustainability concerns in large language model development.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Modern Warfare 4 Official News and Platform Details Emerge
items.summary: First official information about the next Call of Duty game (rumored to be Modern Warfare 4) has been revealed, with confirmation that PS4 will not be supported as the franchise drops previous generation console support in favor of significant engine upgrades.

items.title: Resident Evil VR Receives Major Full Body Experience Update
items.summary: The RE Requiem mod for Resident Evil VR has been significantly enhanced with full body presence, physical weapon swapping, physical grenade throwing, and improved performance features.

items.title: Legendary ZSNES Emulator Returns with Complete Rewrite
items.summary: The iconic ZSNES emulator has made an unexpected return after nearly two decades with Super ZNES, a complete ground-up rewrite featuring GPU acceleration technology.

items.title: Toronto Maple Leafs Win NHL Draft Lottery
items.summary: The Toronto Maple Leafs have won the NHL Draft Lottery, with discussion about whether this development will influence star player Auston Matthews' future with the team.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: AMD Reports Strong Q1 Results with 38% Revenue Growth
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices reported Q1 revenue of $10.25B, exceeding estimates, with Data Center revenue jumping 57% to $5.8B. The company also forecasted Q2 revenue above expectations, driving a 7%+ pre-market stock jump.

items.title: Corning and Nvidia Partner on Advanced Manufacturing Plants
items.summary: Nvidia and glassmaker Corning announced a partnership to open three advanced manufacturing plants in North Carolina and Texas dedicated to optical technology, boosting Corning's US capacity by 10 times.

items.title: Nuro Approved for Driverless Robotaxi Testing in California
items.summary: Nuro received approval to test its driverless Uber robotaxis on California roadways, with plans to begin fully autonomous testing later this year.

items.title: Alexa+ Expands Beyond Amazon to Bose Speakers
items.summary: Amazon's upgraded smart assistant Alexa+ is now available on multiple Bose speaker models, marking the service's debut on non-Amazon devices.

items.title: Form Launches Affordable Smart Swimming Goggles
items.summary: Form introduced the Smart Swim 2 LT, a more affordable version of its smart swimming goggles that removes heart-rate monitoring while retaining other features.
